mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that reduces equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points signals improving labor market conditions, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with UNRATE declines > 0.5pp are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 1.0% signals robust supply-side growth, reducing recession risks and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in INDPRO surge periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly acceleration in GDP growth above 3.0% indicates strong demand momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in GDPC1 surge periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.04 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals strong demand conditions, creating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise > 1.0pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods where both HOUST and RSAFS rise > 1.0pp are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in CPI inflation above 2.5% during a monetary tightening cycle triggers central bank rate hikes, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days through higher discount rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with CPI > 2.5% and rate hikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payrolls (PAYEMS) below -100k signals weakening labor demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through recession risk escalation.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in PAYEMS decline periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in residential construction (HOUST) indicates sustained demand for housing, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in HOUST growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.04 over 3-year windows.
